wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the R&D Management System project overview. Explain its purpose as a pharmaceutical research and development workflow management system for PT Herbatech Innopharma Industry. Document the core functionality including raw material formula management, packaging material trials, multi-level approval workflows, and audit trail compliance. Describe the target audience (R&D staff, operational managers, general managers, superadmins) and key business value propositions. Include system architecture overview showing the Laravel-based MVC structure, technology stack summary (PHP 8.3+, Laravel 11.x, Tailwind CSS, Alpine.js), and high-level component relationships. Provide context about the pharmaceutical industry requirements this system addresses.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Formula CRUD operations. Explain the complete create, read, update, and delete functionality including form handling, validation rules, and data persistence. Document the FormulaController methods, request validation, and Blade template interactions. Include practical examples of creating new formulas, editing existing ones, viewing formula details, and managing formula listings. Address form validation, error handling, and user feedback mechanisms.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the user management system. Detail the User model implementation with Spatie Permission traits, user registration and profile management, password hashing and security measures, and user relationships with formulas, trials, and other entities. Document the user CRUD operations, profile updates, password changes, and account deletion processes. Include practical examples of creating users programmatically, managing user attributes, and implementing custom user validation rules. Address user session handling, remember me functionality, and user audit logging integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Raw Material Trials
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Raw Material (RM) Trial Management system. Detail the complete trial lifecycle from creation to approval, including trial data models, parameter verification workflows, and decision recording mechanisms. Explain the relationship between trials and formulas, sample identity tracking, process steps documentation, and multi-level approval processes (Operational Manager → General Manager). Document the verification system with detailed field specifications, validation rules, and business logic. Include practical examples of creating RM trials, managing verifications, handling approvals/rejections, and generating reports. Address audit trail integration, activity logging, and compliance requirements specific to raw material testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blade Templates & Layouts
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Blade templating system and layout architecture. Detail the main application layout structure in app.blade.php including sidebar navigation, header components, and responsive design patterns. Explain template inheritance using @extends directives, section blocks with @yield and @section, and component composition patterns. Document the guest layout for authentication pages and navigation component implementation. Include practical examples of creating new layouts, extending existing templates, and implementing dynamic content sections. Address Blade syntax conventions, conditional rendering with @if/@else directives, loop structures, and integration with Laravel's routing system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Business Entities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for core business entities including Formula, TrialRm, TrialPm, and User models. Detail entity relationships, field definitions, and data types with Eloquent ORM implementation patterns. Document primary/foreign keys, validation rules, and business constraints. Explain the relationship patterns between formulas, trials, and users including parent-child relationships for versioning and approval workflows. Include sample data structures, relationship queries, and performance considerations for complex joins.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for authentication endpoints. Document all authentication-related HTTP methods including login, logout, password reset, email verification, and user registration. Include detailed request/response schemas for each endpoint, authentication token handling, session management, and security considerations. Provide examples of implementing client-side authentication flows, handling authentication errors, and managing user sessions. Document middleware requirements and role-based access control integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Unit Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed unit testing documentation covering the approach for testing individual components in isolation. Document how to write unit tests for Eloquent models, service classes, and policy authorization logic. Explain testing best practices for business logic validation, data transformation, and method assertions. Include practical examples of testing FormulaService calculations, TrialRmService workflows, and user policies. Cover mocking strategies for external dependencies, database interactions using in-memory SQLite, and testing edge cases. Address test organization patterns, naming conventions, and assertion techniques specific to Laravel's testing framework.

wiki_catalogs.name: Server Requirements & Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive server requirements and setup documentation for the R&D Management System. Detail PHP version requirements (8.3+), required extensions, memory limits, and file permissions. Document web server configuration for Apache and Nginx including virtual host setup, URL rewriting rules, and security headers. Include database server requirements for MySQL/SQLite, storage directory permissions, and system-level dependencies. Provide step-by-step installation procedures for different environments including shared hosting, VPS, and cloud platforms.

wiki_catalogs.name: Coding Standards & Conventions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive coding standards documentation covering PHP, Blade templates, JavaScript, and CSS conventions for the R&D Management System. Detail Laravel-specific coding patterns including controller structure, model relationships, service layer implementation, and policy-based authorization. Document Blade template organization, component development patterns, and Tailwind CSS usage guidelines. Include code formatting rules, naming conventions, file organization principles, and architectural patterns like MVC separation and service-oriented design. Provide concrete examples from the actual codebase demonstrating proper implementation of business rules validation, RBAC implementation, and audit trail logging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the core UI components including buttons (primary-button, secondary-button, danger-button) and form elements (text-input, input-label, input-error). Document component props, attributes, styling options, and usage patterns. Include practical examples showing how to use each component with different configurations. Explain the component API, event handling, and customization options. Address accessibility features, responsive behavior, and integration with Alpine.js for interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Raw Material Trial API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Raw Material (RM) trial management endpoints. Document all CRUD operations including trial creation, listing with search and filtering capabilities, detailed view retrieval, editing, and deletion. Include the complete parameter verification system with validation rules for parameters like Warna, Aroma, Rasa, pH, Viskositas, and Berat Jenis. Document the approval submission workflow, status transitions, and decision recording (Lulus/Reformulasi). Provide detailed request/response schemas for trial data structures, verification parameters, and approval workflows. Include authentication requirements, authorization policies, and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for formula CRUD operations. Document the GET /formulas endpoint with search, filtering by status and stage parameters, and pagination support. Detail POST /formulas for creating new formulas with material composition validation, PUT /formulas/{id} for updating existing formulas, and DELETE /formulas/{id} for deletion. Include request/response schemas, validation rules, authentication requirements, and error handling patterns. Provide examples of complex queries with multiple filters and bulk operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Administration API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for user administration endpoints. Document all superadmin-only operations including user creation, editing, role assignment, and deletion. Include HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, validation rules, and authentication requirements. Document the role-based access control integration with Spatie Permission package. Provide examples for creating users with specific roles, updating user information, changing passwords, and removing users from the system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Materials API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for materials management endpoints. Document all CRUD operations including GET /materials, POST /materials, PUT/PATCH /materials/{id}, and DELETE /materials/{id}. Include request/response schemas with validation rules for material name (required, max 150 chars, unique), type (optional, max 50 chars), unit (required, max 20 chars), and description fields. Document pagination support returning 15 items per page, error responses for validation failures and duplicate names, and success response formats. Include examples of creating new materials, updating existing ones, and handling deletion with proper status codes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Environment Setup
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive environment setup documentation for the R&D Management System. Document system requirements including PHP 8.3+, Node.js, and database prerequisites. Explain the complete installation process using Composer dependencies, NPM packages, and Laravel-specific setup commands. Cover environment configuration including .env file setup, database connections, mail configuration, and storage permissions. Include development server setup with concurrent processes (artisan serve, queue worker, Pail logger, Vite dev server). Document common setup issues and troubleshooting steps for different operating systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: PHP Coding Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive PHP coding standards documentation for the R&D Management System. Detail Laravel-specific coding patterns including controller structure following RESTful conventions, Eloquent model relationships and query scopes, service layer implementation with business logic separation, and policy-based authorization patterns. Document naming conventions for classes, methods, variables, and files according to PSR-12 standards. Include code formatting rules enforced by .editorconfig, proper error handling patterns, dependency injection usage, and validation request classes. Provide concrete examples from the actual codebase demonstrating proper implementation of RBAC, audit trail logging, and complex business rule validation.

wiki_catalogs.name: Migration Conventions & Best Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database migration conventions and best practices in the R&D Management System. Document naming conventions for migration files, table structures, column definitions, and index strategies. Include guidelines for foreign key relationships, constraints, and data types selection. Provide examples of complex migrations like polymorphic associations, pivot tables, and soft deletes. Document migration versioning strategies, rollback procedures, and environment-specific configurations. Address performance considerations including indexing strategies, query optimization, and database schema evolution patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Laravel Conventions & Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Laravel-specific coding conventions and patterns used in the R&D Management System. Document controller structure following RESTful resource patterns, service layer implementation with business logic separation, proper dependency injection usage, and request validation patterns. Include examples from FormulaController showing CRUD operations, service method calls, and response formatting. Explain the separation of concerns between controllers (HTTP handling) and services (business logic). Document middleware usage, route model binding, and form request classes. Provide concrete examples of error handling, logging integration, and transaction management patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Structure & Inheritance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for Blade layout structure and inheritance patterns. Document the main application layout hierarchy, template inheritance using @extends and @section directives, and component composition patterns. Explain the sidebar navigation system with role-based menu rendering, responsive design implementation using Tailwind CSS classes, and mobile-first approach. Cover header sections, breadcrumb implementation, flash message handling, and error display patterns. Include examples of extending layouts, defining slots, and passing data to child views.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for the R&D Management System. Include detailed installation requirements (PHP 8.3+, Composer, Node.js, SQLite/MySQL), step-by-step environment setup procedures, database configuration options, and first-time application initialization. Document the complete setup process from cloning the repository to running the application locally. Include environment variable configuration, database migration execution, seed data loading, and basic troubleshooting for common setup issues. Provide clear instructions for both development and production environments with specific commands and configuration examples.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula Versioning System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for the formula versioning system. Detail the parent-child relationship implementation for tracking formula revisions and reformulations. Explain how version numbers are managed, how child formulas inherit from parent formulas, and the historical tracking mechanism. Document the database schema relationships, Eloquent model relationships (parentFormula, childFormulas), and business logic for maintaining formula lineage. Include examples of creating reformulations, navigating formula history, and understanding version hierarchies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Role & Permission System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the role-based access control system using Spatie Permission package. Document the three-tier role hierarchy (Staff R&D, Operational Manager, General Manager, Superadmin) and their specific permissions. Explain permission assignment, role creation and management, policy-based authorization patterns, and middleware implementation. Include practical examples of defining new roles, assigning permissions, protecting routes and actions, and implementing fine-grained access control. Address permission caching, performance optimization, and debugging permission issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Packaging Material Trials
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Packaging Material (PM) Trial Management system. Detail the complete packaging trial workflow including comprehensive trial forms, departmental approval processes, photo documentation handling, and print functionality. Explain the PM trial data model structure, approval status management, and relationship with materials and suppliers. Document the specialized approval workflow with multiple departments, decision recording, and rejection handling. Include practical examples of creating PM trials, uploading photos and documents, managing departmental approvals, and generating printable trial reports. Address file storage management, document versioning, and compliance documentation requirements specific to packaging materials.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for the reusable UI component system built with Blade components. Document all available components including buttons (primary-button, secondary-button, danger-button), form elements (text-input, input-label, input-error), navigation elements (nav-link, dropdown, dropdown-link), and specialized components (modal, status-badge, empty-state). Explain component creation patterns, props passing, slot usage, and event handling with Alpine.js integration. Include practical examples of creating custom components, component composition, and styling approaches. Address component naming conventions, accessibility considerations, and responsive design patterns used throughout the component library.

wiki_catalogs.name: Master Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for master data entities including Material, Supplier, and Setting models. Document entity relationships, field definitions, and data types used for reference data management. Explain how these models support the core business operations through foreign key relationships and lookup functionality. Include data validation rules, business constraints, and usage patterns for maintaining consistent reference data across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for formula management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for formulas, material composition management, version control operations, and approval workflow submissions. Include request/response schemas for formula creation, editing, submission for approval, and reformulation processes. Document file upload handling for formula documents, validation rules, and error responses. Provide examples of complex formula operations including material composition updates and version tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Testing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive feature testing documentation focusing on end-to-end user workflow testing. Detail how to test complete user journeys including authentication flows, formula creation workflows, trial management processes, and approval center operations. Explain controller testing with HTTP requests, response assertions, and status code verification. Cover testing of middleware functionality, route protection, and role-based access control. Include examples of testing complex business scenarios like multi-step approval processes, file uploads, and form submissions. Document testing strategies for API endpoints, session handling, and database state verification after feature execution.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed environment configuration documentation covering .env file setup, environment-specific configurations, and application settings management. Document all critical environment variables including APP_KEY generation, database connections, mail configuration, and cache settings. Explain configuration caching strategies, environment detection mechanisms, and secure secret management. Include examples of development, staging, and production environment configurations with security best practices.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Workflow & Git Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for development workflow and Git practices for the R&D Management System. Document the branching strategy, commit message conventions, pull request process, and code review guidelines. Explain the development environment setup including Composer dependencies, NPM packages, database migrations, and seeders. Cover the testing workflow with PHPUnit tests, feature testing strategies, and test data management using factories and seeders. Include deployment procedures, environment configuration, and continuous integration considerations. Address debugging techniques, logging practices, and performance profiling tools available in the Laravel ecosystem.

wiki_catalogs.name: Navigation & Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for navigation and layout components including nav-link, dropdown, dropdown-link, and layout components (AppLayout, GuestLayout). Document component hierarchy, slot usage, and state management. Explain how to create responsive navigation menus, dropdown menus with Alpine.js interactivity, and layout inheritance patterns. Include examples of implementing user authentication layouts, navigation state management, and mobile-responsive design patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Packaging Material Trial API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for Packaging Material (PM) trial management endpoints. Document trial creation with comprehensive form data including specifications, execution details, and departmental approvals. Include photo and document upload functionality, multi-level approval workflow from Operational Manager to General Manager, and batch approval/rejection capabilities. Document the complete trial lifecycle with state management, approval history tracking, and audit trail integration. Provide detailed schemas for PM trial data structures, approval requests, rejection reasons, and attachment handling. Include authentication requirements, role-based access control, and comprehensive error handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula Versioning & Reformulation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for formula versioning and reformulation endpoints. Document the POST /formulas/{id}/reformulate endpoint that creates new versions based on existing formulas, maintaining parent-child relationships. Detail the version tracking system, automatic code generation for new versions, and data inheritance from parent formulas. Include request/response schemas for reformulation operations, version history retrieval, and comparison between formula versions. Provide examples of version branching workflows and rollback scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Profile Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for user profile management endpoints. Document personal profile update operations including name changes, email updates, password modifications, and signature file uploads. Include request/response schemas, validation rules, file upload handling, and security considerations. Document the ProfileUpdateRequest validation class and its integration with the User model. Provide examples for updating profile information, changing passwords securely, and managing user signature files.

wiki_catalogs.name: Suppliers API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for suppliers management endpoints. Document all CRUD operations including GET /suppliers, POST /suppliers, PUT/PATCH /suppliers/{id}, and DELETE /suppliers/{id}. Include request/response schemas with field validation rules, data types, and constraints. Document search and filtering capabilities, pagination support, and relationship management with materials. Include examples of supplier creation, updates, deletions, and querying with proper HTTP status codes and error handling patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Version Control & Git Practices
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for version control and Git practices in the R&D Management System. Document the branching strategy including feature branches, release branches, and hotfix workflows. Explain commit message conventions following conventional commits format. Cover pull request procedures including code review guidelines, testing requirements before merging, and approval processes. Include branch naming conventions, merge strategies, and conflict resolution procedures. Document the .gitignore patterns specific to Laravel development and explain what files should never be committed to version control.

wiki_catalogs.name: Blade Template Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed Blade template coding standards documentation covering layout inheritance patterns, component development guidelines, and view organization principles. Document proper use of Blade directives, conditional rendering, loop optimization, and data binding patterns. Include Tailwind CSS integration standards, responsive design conventions, and accessibility compliance requirements. Provide examples of reusable components like status badges, approval timelines, and form elements. Document asset management with Vite, CSS organization, and JavaScript integration patterns using Alpine.js for interactive elements.

wiki_catalogs.name: Eloquent Model Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for Eloquent model standards and conventions used throughout the R&D Management System. Document model naming conventions, property definitions, relationship declarations, and accessor/mutator patterns. Include guidelines for model validation rules, scope definitions, and event handling. Provide examples of complex relationships like hasManyThrough, morphTo, and custom relationship methods. Document model attributes casting, date formatting, and serialization patterns. Address model testing strategies, factory integration, and performance optimization techniques including eager loading and query optimization.

wiki_catalogs.name: Naming Conventions
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document comprehensive naming conventions for the R&D Management System following PSR-12 standards. Detail class naming patterns for Models, Controllers, Services, Policies, and Request classes. Explain variable naming conventions including camelCase for variables, PascalCase for classes, and snake_case for database columns. Document file organization patterns, method naming conventions, and constant definitions. Include examples from User model, Formula policy, and ProfileUpdateRequest showing proper naming implementation. Address namespace organization, alias usage, and import statement ordering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive component development guidelines for reusable Blade components. Document component creation patterns, prop passing conventions, and slot usage. Explain the status badge component implementation with dynamic styling based on state values, audit trail component for activity logging display, approval timeline visualization, and modal dialog patterns. Include best practices for component organization, naming conventions, and reusability principles. Provide examples of creating custom components with proper validation, default values, and accessibility compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the authentication and authorization system. Detail the role-based access control implementation using Spatie Permission package, user registration and login flows, password management, and email verification processes. Document the three-tier role hierarchy (Staff R&D, Operational Manager, General Manager, Superadmin) and their specific permissions. Explain the custom RoleMiddleware implementation, policy-based authorization patterns, and security considerations. Include practical examples of implementing new roles, adding permissions, and protecting routes. Address session management, password reset functionality, and audit logging integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Material Composition Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for material composition management within formulas. Explain how materials are linked to formulas through the FormulaMaterial model, percentage calculations, and composition validation. Document the material selection process, quantity calculations, and the automatic total percentage validation ensuring compositions sum to 100%. Include practical examples of adding materials to formulas, updating quantities, handling material changes, and validating composition integrity.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication Middleware
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for authentication middleware implementation. Detail the custom RoleMiddleware for route protection, middleware registration in bootstrap configuration, and route-level authorization patterns. Document how to protect routes with roles and permissions, implement conditional middleware logic, and handle unauthorized access scenarios. Include practical examples of creating custom middleware, chaining multiple middleware, and testing middleware functionality. Address middleware performance considerations and error handling strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trial Verification System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Trial Verification System that handles parameter validation and quality checks for raw material trials. Explain the verification data model, validation rules, and business logic for parameter checking. Document the verification workflow including who can perform verifications, required parameters, pass/fail criteria, and escalation procedures. Detail the integration with trial management, status updates, and audit trail generation. Include practical examples of creating verifications, handling validation failures, managing re-verifications, and generating verification reports. Address data integrity constraints, validation error handling, and compliance requirements for pharmaceutical testing.

wiki_catalogs.name: Styling & Design System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the styling architecture using Tailwind CSS and custom design tokens. Detail the Tailwind configuration setup, custom color palette, typography system, and spacing utilities. Explain the CSS architecture organization, custom utility classes, and design system implementation. Document responsive design patterns, mobile-first approach, and cross-browser compatibility considerations. Include practical examples of implementing consistent styling, creating custom components with Tailwind classes, and maintaining design consistency across the application. Address performance optimization techniques, CSS purging, and asset compilation with Vite.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Workflow Schema
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for approval workflow entities including TrialPmApproval and TrialRmVerification models. Detail the multi-level approval process schema, field definitions for approval states, timestamps, and audit information. Document the relationship patterns between trial entities and their corresponding approval records. Explain the state machine implementation through database constraints and status tracking fields. Include query patterns for approval history retrieval and workflow progression analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trial Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for trial management endpoints covering both Raw Material (RM) and Packaging Material (PM) trials. Document trial creation, parameter verification, approval workflows, and status management. Include detailed schemas for trial data structures, approval requests, rejection reasons, and photo/document uploads. Document the complete trial lifecycle from creation to final approval or rejection.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Trail & Activity Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed data model documentation for audit trail and activity logging system. Document the activity log table structure, event tracking fields, and user action recording mechanisms. Explain how Spatie Activity Log package integrates with custom models to provide comprehensive change tracking. Document the relationship between activity logs and business entities, including batch processing support and event categorization. Include query patterns for compliance reporting and audit analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Center API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for approval center endpoints. Document batch approval/rejection operations, approval history retrieval, and notification triggers. Include request/response schemas for approval actions, rejection reasons, and approval status queries. Document role-based access controls for different approval levels and provide examples of implementing approval workflows in client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Configuration & CI/CD
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed testing configuration and continuous integration documentation covering PHPUnit setup, environment configuration, and CI/CD pipeline integration. Explain phpunit.xml configuration options, test suite organization, and source code coverage settings. Document environment variable configuration for testing, database connection setup, and service mocking in test environments. Include guidance on setting up GitHub Actions or other CI/CD pipelines for automated test execution. Cover test reporting, coverage analysis tools, and performance benchmarking. Address debugging failing tests, parallel test execution, and optimizing test suite performance for faster feedback loops.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment Strategies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed deployment strategy documentation covering various deployment approaches for the R&D Management System. Document traditional server deployment, containerized deployment with Docker, and cloud platform deployment patterns. Include CI/CD pipeline setup, automated testing integration, and zero-downtime deployment strategies. Address rollback procedures, health checks, and monitoring integration for production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component Development Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive guidelines for creating custom Blade components following the project's established patterns. Document component creation workflow, naming conventions, prop definitions, slot usage, and event handling patterns. Explain integration with Tailwind CSS for styling, Alpine.js for interactivity, and Laravel's view component system. Include best practices for component composition, testing strategies, and performance optimization. Provide step-by-step examples of building reusable components that follow the existing codebase architecture and design system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Formula Material Composition
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for formula material composition management. Document how materials are added, updated, and removed from formulas through the materials array structure in create/update operations. Detail validation rules for material percentages (must sum to 100%), supplier associations, and material existence checks. Include request/response schemas for material composition updates, batch material operations, and material validation errors. Provide examples of complex material compositions and percentage calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Troubleshooting & Debugging Guide
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed troubleshooting and debugging guide for common issues in the R&D Management System. Document debugging techniques for Laravel applications including log analysis, query debugging, and performance profiling. Cover database connection issues, migration problems, and permission-related errors. Include guidance on debugging authentication and authorization issues, approval workflow problems, and file upload failures. Address common deployment issues, environment configuration problems, and third-party integration troubleshooting. Provide step-by-step solutions for frequent development and production issues with specific error messages and their resolutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Debugging & Performance Profiling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for debugging and performance profiling in the R&D Management System. Document Laravel debugging tools including Telescope integration, error logging, and stack traces. Explain database query debugging, N+1 query detection, and performance monitoring. Cover memory usage profiling, execution time analysis, and bottleneck identification. Include debugging techniques for authentication flows, approval workflows, and complex business logic. Document logging best practices, log rotation, and production debugging considerations. Address common performance issues and optimization strategies specific to the application architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: CSS & Tailwind CSS Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create CSS and Tailwind CSS coding standards documentation covering utility-first CSS conventions, custom theme configuration, and responsive design patterns. Document color palette usage, typography scales, spacing systems, and component styling approaches. Include guidelines for creating custom Tailwind utilities, managing CSS assets with Vite, and optimizing build output. Provide examples of consistent styling patterns used throughout the application, mobile-first responsive design implementation, and accessibility considerations in CSS styling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authorization & Policy Patterns
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document authorization patterns and RBAC implementation using Laravel policies and Spatie Permission package. Detail policy class structure, authorization methods, and role-based access control patterns. Include examples from FormulaPolicy showing create, update, delete, and view authorization logic. Explain middleware implementation for route protection, policy gates, and permission checking in views. Document user role assignment, permission hierarchy, and dynamic authorization rules. Provide examples of conditional authorization based on model ownership and workflow states.

wiki_catalogs.name: Asset Management & Vite Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Document asset management strategies and Vite build tool integration for optimal performance. Explain JavaScript module bundling with Alpine.js integration, CSS compilation pipeline, and asset versioning strategies. Cover image optimization, font loading strategies, and CDN configuration. Document the Vite configuration setup, development workflow, and production build optimization. Include examples of importing assets in Blade templates, managing dependencies, and implementing lazy loading for better user experience.

wiki_catalogs.name: Trial Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Trial Management system covering both Raw Material (RM) and Packaging Material (PM) trials. Detail the trial creation workflows, parameter verification systems, decision recording mechanisms, and departmental approval processes. Explain the differences between RM and PM trial types, their specific data models, and unique business rules. Document the service layer implementations, controller logic, and view templates. Include practical examples of creating trials, managing verifications, handling approvals, and generating reports. Address file upload capabilities for photos and documents, print functionality, and audit trail integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Migrations & Version Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for database migration strategy and version control practices. Document the migration naming conventions, rollback procedures, and environment-specific configurations. Explain the evolution of schema changes over time, including backward compatibility considerations and data transformation strategies. Document database connection settings, indexing strategies, and performance optimization techniques implemented through migrations. Include guidelines for creating new migrations and handling production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Master Data API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for master data management endpoints. Document CRUD operations for materials and suppliers, including data validation rules, relationship management, and bulk operations. Include request/response schemas for creating, updating, and querying master data entities. Document search and filtering capabilities, data import/export endpoints, and reference data maintenance operations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Migration & Schema Standards
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create database migration and schema design standards documentation covering Eloquent model conventions, relationship definitions, and migration best practices. Document naming conventions for tables, columns, indexes, and foreign keys. Include guidelines for data validation at the database level, constraint definitions, and migration versioning strategies. Provide examples of complex relationships like polymorphic associations, pivot tables, and soft deletes. Document factory patterns for test data generation, seeders for initial data, and database performance optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Approval Center
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Approval Center system. Detail the centralized dashboard for managing pending approvals across all trial types and formula submissions. Explain the batch approval/rejection capabilities, approval history tracking, and notification system integration. Document the approval workflow states, user interface components, and backend processing logic. Include practical examples of handling approvals, viewing approval timelines, and managing approval queues. Address the integration with activity logging for audit compliance and the role-based access controls for different approval levels.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed API documentation for user management and profile operations. Document user CRUD operations, role assignment, permission management, and profile updates. Include request/response schemas for user creation, role modification, password changes, and profile information updates. Document superadmin-only endpoints and integration with the role-based permission system.

wiki_catalogs.name: Master Data Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Master Data Management system covering materials and suppliers. Detail the complete CRUD operations for maintaining reference data, including material catalog management and supplier information systems. Explain the relationships between master data and other modules like formulas and trials. Document data validation rules, import/export capabilities, and data integrity constraints. Include practical examples of managing materials, organizing suppliers, and maintaining data consistency across the application. Address the factory pattern usage for test data generation and best practices for master data maintenance.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Settings API
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for system settings endpoints. Document configuration management operations, company branding customization, and application preferences. Include request/response schemas for retrieving, updating, and managing system-wide settings. Document superadmin-only access controls and provide examples of implementing dynamic configuration in client applications.

wiki_catalogs.name: User Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the User Management system. Detail the user administration interface, role assignment and management capabilities, and permission configuration tools. Explain the user lifecycle from creation through profile management to deactivation. Document the integration with the role-based access control system and how users inherit permissions from their assigned roles. Include practical examples of creating users, assigning roles, managing user profiles, and performing bulk user operations. Address the seeder system for initial user setup and role-permission configurations.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Settings
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the System Settings module. Detail the application configuration interface, company branding customization options, and system preferences management. Explain the settings data model, storage mechanism, and how settings are accessed throughout the application. Document the available configuration categories, validation rules for settings values, and security considerations for sensitive configurations. Include practical examples of adding new settings, updating existing ones, and accessing settings in controllers and views. Address the settings persistence strategy and backup considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Audit Trail and Compliance system. Detail the activity logging implementation using Spatie Activity Log package, change tracking mechanisms, and compliance reporting capabilities. Explain how activities are logged across different modules, the audit data structure, and query patterns for retrieving historical changes. Document the audit trail visualization components, filtering capabilities, and export functionality. Include practical examples of implementing custom activity logging, generating compliance reports, and analyzing audit data for regulatory requirements. Address data retention policies and performance considerations for large audit datasets.

wiki_catalogs.name: Frontend Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the frontend architecture and UI components. Detail the Blade templating structure, component system implementation, and styling approach using Tailwind CSS. Explain the layout inheritance patterns, reusable component development, and interactive elements powered by Alpine.js. Document the asset compilation pipeline with Vite, responsive design patterns, and accessibility considerations. Include practical examples of creating custom components, extending layouts, and implementing interactive features. Address the component naming conventions, styling guidelines, and performance optimization techniques for the frontend layer.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive API documentation for all web endpoints and request/response patterns. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request parameters, response schemas, and authentication requirements for each endpoint. Include detailed specifications for form submissions, file uploads, and approval workflow endpoints. Document error response formats, validation error structures, and status codes used throughout the application. Include practical examples of making requests, handling responses, and implementing client-side integrations. Address rate limiting, security headers, and CORS configuration where applicable.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Schema Design
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive database schema documentation detailing entity relationships, field definitions, and data types across all tables. Document primary/foreign keys, indexes, constraints, and data validation rules implemented at the database level. Explain the relationship patterns between core entities like Formulas, Trials, Users, and supporting master data. Include database schema diagrams, sample data structures, and migration strategies. Document data access patterns, Eloquent relationships, and performance considerations including indexing strategies and query optimization techniques.

wiki_catalogs.name: Testing Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive testing documentation covering the testing strategy, framework setup, and best practices. Detail the unit testing approach for models and services, feature testing for user workflows, and integration testing for external dependencies. Explain the test data management using factories and seeders, test database configuration, and mocking strategies. Include practical examples of writing tests for controllers, services, and business logic. Document continuous integration setup, test coverage requirements, and debugging techniques for failing tests. Address performance testing considerations and test data isolation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and configuration documentation covering production setup, environment configuration, and performance optimization. Detail server requirements, web server configuration (Apache/Nginx), PHP-FPM settings, and database optimization for production environments. Document environment-specific configurations, caching strategies, queue worker setup, and monitoring integration. Include practical deployment steps, SSL certificate configuration, backup strategies, and disaster recovery procedures. Address scaling considerations, load balancing setup, and cloud deployment patterns for the Laravel application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Developer Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive developer guidelines covering code style conventions, development workflow, contribution guidelines, and best practices. Detail the coding standards for PHP, Blade templates, JavaScript, and CSS following Laravel conventions. Explain the Git workflow, branching strategies, pull request process, and code review guidelines. Document the UI/UX design principles, component development patterns, and accessibility requirements. Include troubleshooting guides for common development issues, debugging techniques, and performance profiling tools. Address the development environment setup, tooling recommendations, and productivity tips for efficient development.

<blog>

# R&D Management System - Comprehensive Project Analysis

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The R&D Management System is a comprehensive web application designed for pharmaceutical research and development operations at PT Herbatech Innopharma Industry. It provides a structured workflow for managing raw material (RM) formulations, packaging material (PM) trials, and approval processes with multi-level authorization.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Streamlined Formula Management**: Track raw material formulations with version control and approval workflows
- **Trial Management**: Manage both raw material and packaging material trials with detailed documentation
- **Multi-Level Approval**: Implement hierarchical approval processes involving Operational Managers and General Managers
- **Audit Trail**: Maintain comprehensive activity logging for compliance and traceability
- **Role-Based Access Control**: Ensure proper authorization through role-based permissions

### Target Audience
- Research & Development Staff
- Operational Managers
- General Managers  
- System Administrators
- Quality Assurance Personnel

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The system follows Laravel's MVC architecture with service layer pattern:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Client Browser] --> B[Laravel Framework]
    B --> C[Controllers Layer]
    C --> D[Service Layer]
    D --> E[Models Layer]
    E --> F[(Database)]
    
    G[Middleware] --> H[Authentication & Authorization]
    I[Blade Templates] --> J[Tailwind CSS + Alpine.js]
    K[Activity Logger] --> L[Audit Trail]
    M[Permission System] --> N[Role-Based Access]
```

### Design Patterns
- **MVC Architecture**: Clear separation of concerns between models, views, and controllers
- **Service Layer Pattern**: Business logic encapsulated in dedicated service classes
- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through Eloquent models
- **Policy Pattern**: Authorization logic centralized in policy classes
- **Factory Pattern**: Test data generation using database factories

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant U as User
    participant C as Controller
    participant S as Service
    participant M as Model
    participant DB as Database
    
    U->>+C: Submit Formula
    C->>+S: submitForApproval()
    S->>+M: Validate & Update Status
    M->>+DB: Save Changes
    DB-->>-M: Confirmation
    M-->>-S: Updated Model
    S-->>-C: Success Response
    C-->>-U: Redirect with Success Message
```

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Application Bootstrap**: `bootstrap/app.php` - Laravel application initialization
- **Route Definitions**: `routes/web.php` - Web route configuration with middleware groups
- **Service Provider**: `app/Providers/AppServiceProvider.php` - Application service registration

### Core Modules

#### Authentication & Authorization
- **User Model**: `app/Models/User.php` - Enhanced with roles and relationships
- **Role Middleware**: `app/Http/Middleware/RoleMiddleware.php` - Custom role-based access control
- **Policies**: `app/Policies/` - Authorization policies for CRUD operations

#### Business Logic Services
- **Formula Service**: `app/Services/FormulaService.php` - Complex formula management logic
- **Trial Services**: `app/Services/TrialRmService.php`, `app/Services/TrialPmService.php` - Trial management workflows

#### Data Models
- **Core Entities**: `app/Models/` - Eloquent models with relationships and business rules
- **Versioning Support**: Formula model supports parent-child relationships for version tracking

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Configuration**: `config/app.php` - Application-wide settings
- **Package Configurations**: Individual config files for third-party packages
- **Database Schema**: Migration files in `database/migrations/` for schema evolution

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies defined in `composer.json`:
- **Laravel Framework**: Core framework providing MVC structure
- **Spatie Permission**: Role and permission management
- **Spatie Activity Log**: Audit trail functionality
- **Barryvdh DomPDF**: PDF generation capabilities
- **Maatwebsite Excel**: Excel import/export functionality

### Integration Points
- **File Storage**: `storage/app/public/signatures/` - Document storage for approvals
- **Email System**: Mail configuration for notifications
- **Queue System**: Background job processing support

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class User {
        +formulas()
        +trialRms()
        +trialPms()
        +hasRole()
    }
    
    class Formula {
        +materials()
        +parentFormula()
        +childFormulas()
        +getIsValidCompositionAttribute()
    }
    
    class TrialRm {
        +formula()
        +verifications()
    }
    
    class TrialPm {
        +departmentApprovals()
        +getIsFullyApprovedByDepartmentsAttribute()
    }
    
    User "1" --> "many" Formula
    User "1" --> "many" TrialRm
    User "1" --> "many" TrialPm
    Formula "1" --> "many" TrialRm
    Formula "1" --> "many" Formula : parent-child
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview

#### Formula Management
- **CRUD Operations**: Complete create, read, update, delete functionality for formulas
- **Version Control**: Automatic versioning with parent-child relationships
- **Material Composition**: Multi-material formulas with percentage validation
- **Status Workflow**: Draft → Pending Tahap 1 → Pending Tahap 2 → Approved/Rejected

#### Trial Management
- **Raw Material Trials**: Detailed trial documentation with parameter verification
- **Packaging Material Trials**: Comprehensive PM trial management with departmental approvals
- **Approval Workflows**: Multi-stage approval processes with audit trails

#### Approval Center
- **Centralized Dashboard**: Single interface for all pending approvals
- **Batch Processing**: Ability to approve/reject multiple items
- **Notification System**: Real-time notification counts in navigation

### Implementation Highlights

#### Advanced Validation
- **Composition Validation**: Ensures formula materials sum to exactly 100%
- **Status Transitions**: Enforces valid state transitions in approval workflows
- **Business Rules**: Complex validation logic encapsulated in service layer

#### Audit & Compliance
- **Activity Logging**: Comprehensive audit trail using Spatie Activity Log
- **Change Tracking**: Tracks who made changes and when
- **Rejection Notes**: Mandatory notes for rejected items

#### User Interface
- **Responsive Design**: Mobile-first design using Tailwind CSS
- **Interactive Components**: Alpine.js for dynamic UI interactions
- **Role-Based Navigation**: Dynamic sidebar based on user permissions

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Draft
    Draft --> Pending_Tahap_1: Submit for Approval
    Pending_Tahap_1 --> Pending_Tahap_2: OM Approval
    Pending_Tahap_1 --> Rejected: OM Rejection
    Pending_Tahap_2 --> Approved: GM Approval
    Pending_Tahap_2 --> Rejected: GM Rejection
    Rejected --> Draft: Reformulate
    Approved --> Draft: Create New Version
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The R&D Management System demonstrates a well-structured Laravel application with clear separation of concerns, robust business logic encapsulation, and comprehensive audit capabilities. The service layer pattern effectively handles complex business rules while maintaining clean controller interfaces.

### Architectural Strengths
- **Scalable Service Layer**: Business logic properly separated from presentation concerns
- **Comprehensive Security**: Multi-layered security with authentication, authorization, and audit trails
- **Flexible Data Modeling**: Strong relationships and versioning support
- **Modern Frontend**: Responsive design with interactive components

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **API Development**: Consider implementing RESTful API endpoints for mobile applications
- **Testing Coverage**: Expand unit and feature test coverage for critical business logic
- **Performance Optimization**: Implement caching strategies for frequently accessed data
- **Documentation**: Add comprehensive API documentation and user guides

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement API Layer**: Create API resources for external integrations
2. **Enhance Testing**: Add comprehensive test suites for service layer methods
3. **Add Caching**: Implement Redis caching for frequently accessed data
4. **Monitor Performance**: Add performance monitoring and logging
5. **Mobile App**: Consider developing companion mobile applications

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement reporting and analytics dashboards
- **Workflow Automation**: Add automated notifications and reminders
- **Integration Capabilities**: Connect with ERP or inventory management systems
- **Advanced Search**: Implement full-text search across all entities
- **Export Capabilities**: Enhance data export functionality with custom templates

Sources:
- [composer.json](composer.json)
- [package.json](package.json)
- [routes/web.php](routes/web.php)
- [app/Models/User.php](app/Models/User.php)
- [app/Models/Formula.php](app/Models/Formula.php)
- [app/Models/TrialRm.php](app/Models/TrialRm.php)
- [app/Models/TrialPm.php](app/Models/TrialPm.php)
- [app/Services/FormulaService.php](app/Services/FormulaService.php)
- [app/Http/Controllers/FormulaController.php](app/Http/Controllers/FormulaController.php)
- [app/Http/Middleware/RoleMiddleware.php](app/Http/Middleware/RoleMiddleware.php)
- [config/app.php](config/app.php)
- [resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php](resources/views/layouts/app.blade.php)
- [database/migrations/2026_07_01_092832_create_formulas_table.php](database/migrations/2026_07_01_092832_create_formulas_table.php)
- [database/migrations/2026_07_01_092840_create_formula_materials_table.php](database/migrations/2026_07_01_092840_create_formula_materials_table.php)
- [database/migrations/2026_07_01_092857_create_trial_rm_verifications_table.php](database/migrations/2026_07_01_092857_create_trial_rm_verifications_table.php)

</blog>
